q: Blood glucose rises after a meal. Which hormonal response is correct?
options: Alpha cells of the islets of Langerhans release glucagon, causing glycogen breakdown
The hypothalamus releases insulin directly into the bloodstream
Beta cells of the islets of Langerhans release insulin, promoting glucose uptake and glycogen synthesis
The adrenal gland releases epinephrine to stimulate glucose absorption in the intestine
explanation: High blood glucose stimulates pancreatic beta cells to release insulin, which causes cells (especially liver and muscle) to take up glucose and convert it to glycogen.
